questions.question: In a group photo of a family, there are grandfather, grandmother, two fathers, two mothers, four children, three grandchildren, one brother, two sisters, two sons, two daughters, one father-in-law, one mother-in-law, one daughter-in-law. How many people are in the photo?
questions.explanation: This classic puzzle: a family of grandfather, grandmother, son (father), daughter-in-law (mother), and their two children (grandchildren). That's just 7 people: grandfather(1), grandmother(1), their son(1) who is a father and son, daughter-in-law(1) who is a mother and mother-in-law, their son(1) who is a brother, their two daughters(2) who are sisters, children, grandchildren. Total = 7 people covering all those roles.

questions.question:
Find the missing number in the matrix:
9  3  6
25 5 20
49 7  ?
Options:
questions.explanation: Pattern: Column 1 are perfect squares: 9=3², 25=5², 49=7². Column 2 are their square roots: 3, 5, 7. Column 3: 9-3=6, 25-5=20, 49-7=42. So ? = 49-7 = 42.

questions.question: A man is looking at a photograph and says, 'The father of this man's son is my father's only son.' Who is in the photograph?
questions.explanation: My father's only son = me. The father of this man's son = this man himself (a man is the father of his own son). So 'this man' = 'my father's only son' = me. But wait: 'the father of this man's son' = this man. 'This man' = my father's only son = me. So the man in the photo is me (the speaker). But looking at options: Son would make sense if the speaker is looking at his father? Let me re-read: 'The father of this man's son' = this man (he is the father). 'My father's only son' = me. So this man = me. Alternatively: 'his son's father' = himself (circular). The man in photo = the speaker himself. Among options, 'Son' would be if the speaker were talking about his own son. Standard version of this puzzle answer: the man in the photo is the speaker's SON. Re-reading: 'father of this man's son' = this man. 'My father's only son' = me. So 'this man' = me. I am the one in the photograph... but that means 'Son' in options refers to the speaker's self-image? The photo shows the speaker's son if: this man = me, and I am talking about myself (circular). Classic answer for this riddle: it's the speaker's own son.
